## v6.2.0

### Fixed
- **Retention stopped pruning an item's older snapshots after its backup path set changed** (#91). Retention was applied per restic *paths group*: when an item's backed-up path list changed for any reason (folder selection saved in the UI, a container update adding/removing an appdata mount, a temporarily missing path), all newer snapshots formed a new group — and the old group, never backed up again, kept its keep-set forever. The per-item snapshot list shows both groups, so pruning looked stopped while the repo kept growing. Retention is now **identity-stable**: it is scoped to each item's stable tag (`container:<name>`, `vm:<name>`, `fileset:<name>`, `flash`, `config`) with grouping disabled, so an item's whole history is one group regardless of path or host changes. **Existing frozen snapshots are cleaned up automatically on the next backup or manual prune** — every snapshot has carried its identity tag from day one, no manual action needed.
- Manual **Prune** and the **off-site retention** pass use the same identity-stable, per-item policy (one prune at the end instead of per item).

### Changed
- **Retention failures now notify.** A failed post-backup retention prune was previously only written to the container log — invisible while the repo silently grew. It now raises a notification (and Unraid alert, if enabled); the backup itself is still never failed by a prune error.
